title: Actor Resolver Null
description: Fallback identity provider when no other provider is detected or selected.
context: Fallback identity provider when no other provider is detected or selected.

rules: The resulting resolver MUST reject `identity()` calls with an error indicating that no identity source is available.

A `NullAutoDetectActorProvider` MUST be registered in the actor provider registry with `name: "null"`. Its `detect()` method MUST always return `null` — there is no environment to detect for the null provider. The `create()` method MUST return a `NullActorResolver`.

This provider is distinct from the `NullActorResolver` fallback: the resolver is the concrete implementation returned when all detection fails; the provider is the registered factory that produces it.

scenarios.name: Rejection
when: identity() is called on a NullActorResolver

scenarios.name: Provider registered with correct name
when: the actor provider registry is inspected
then: a provider named "null" exists and implements AutoDetectActorProvider

scenarios.name: Provider create() returns NullActorResolver
when: create() is called on the null provider
then: it returns an instance of NullActorResolver (or something satisfying ActorResolver)

scenarios.name: Provider detect() always returns null
when: detect(cwd) is called on the null provider
then: it returns null for any cwd
